free_text: Excitation functions for deuteron induced reactions in
natural nickel: production of no-carrier-added Cu-64
from enriched Ni-64 targets for positron emission
tomography
main reference
, Nuffield cyclotron, Birmingham
description of
target assembly and irradiation conditions.
description of
target assembly and irradiation conditions.
Decay data were
taken from this article.
- The energy
degradation was calculated using tables from this
article.
Natural nickel foils (thickness 50 micron,
purity 99.99%). Eight foils were put in one stack
and covered with 50 micron aluminum foil. Three stacks
were prepared in this way. The enriched Ni-64 material
was in powder form, and was pressed into 5 mm diam.
pellets. The average target thickness was 104 mg/cm2.
the Ni-64 targets also were covered with 50 micron
aluminun or 25 micron tantalum foils.
isotopic composition of the enriched material in %
material1           material2
excitation functions were measured via the
activation technique using stacked foil targets.
Three stacks, each containing 8 nickel samples were
irradiated from 8 to 30 min with a beam current of 1-3
micro-A of deuteron particles.
errors in the cross section data were
estimated by taking the square root of the sum of the
squares of the individual relative errors.
The main error sources were:
uncertainty in beam current measurement
uncertainty in foil thickness
uncertainty in activity determination
including the errors of detector eff., counting
statistics, counting geometry and decay data
data were taken from tables 3-4 of Applied
Radiation and Isotopes, vol. 42, pp. 193-197, 1991.
